tasks.title: Author writing.md and writing/voice.md
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research current best practices for this discipline. Search for official guidelines, established frameworks, community conventions, and recent methodological changes. Do not write from training data alone.

Create two files:

1. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/writing.md — The writing discipline class prompt. 30-60 lines. Covers: reader-first structure, concrete examples over abstract description, scannable formatting, progressive disclosure (lead with the answer, details follow), tone calibration. Must include a reference to writing/voice.md for voice-specific guidance (e.g., 'For voice and tone guidance, see the voice prompt loaded alongside this one' or similar). Research current technical writing best practices (Google developer docs style guide, Microsoft writing style guide, etc.).

2. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/writing/voice.md — Voice guidance separated so users can override it independently. 20-40 lines. Covers: default voice characteristics (clear, direct, human), when to adjust formality, how to handle jargon, audience awareness. This file exists so organizations can drop in their own voice guide without touching the writing discipline prompt.

ZERO wolfcastle-specific content in either file.

tasks.title: Author research.md and architecture.md
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research current best practices for this discipline. Search for official guidelines, established frameworks, community conventions, and recent methodological changes. Do not write from training data alone.

Create two non-language class prompts:

1. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/research.md — Research/information gathering discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: source citation and verification, accuracy over speed, structured output format, distinguishing primary from secondary sources, handling conflicting information, when to stop researching and start synthesizing. Research best practices from library science, competitive analysis frameworks, and systematic review methodology.

2. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/architecture.md — System design discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: dependency analysis, failure mode thinking, decomposition principles, tradeoff documentation, constraint identification, how to evaluate alternatives (not just list them), when to write ADRs. Research current architecture decision frameworks (C4 model, arc42, etc.).

ZERO wolfcastle-specific content. No references to wolfcastle commands, markers, or system mechanics.

tasks.title: Author audit.md and testing.md
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research current best practices for this discipline. Search for official guidelines, established frameworks, community conventions, and recent methodological changes. Do not write from training data alone.

Create two non-language class prompts:

1. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/audit.md — Verification and review discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: read-only review stance (do not fix, only record), gap identification and recording, verification checklist methodology, evidence-based findings, distinguishing severity levels, coverage analysis, how to handle ambiguous findings. The audit class is special: it emphasizes observation and recording over action.

2. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/testing.md — Test suite creation discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: coverage strategy (behavior over implementation), fixture design, test naming conventions, flaky test prevention, test isolation, boundary testing, property-based testing awareness, performance test considerations. Research current testing frameworks and methodologies (TDD, BDD, property testing).

ZERO wolfcastle-specific content.

tasks.title: Author design.md, devops.md, data.md, and security.md
tasks.description: Author design.md, devops.md, data.md, and security.md
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research current best practices for this discipline. Search for official guidelines, established frameworks, community conventions, and recent methodological changes. Do not write from training data alone.

Create four non-language class prompts:

1. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/design.md — UI/UX design discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: user goal identification, interaction sequence thinking, edge state handling (empty, error, loading, overflow), accessibility considerations, responsive design awareness, design system consistency. Research current design system practices (Material Design 3, Apple HIG).

2. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/devops.md — Infrastructure and CI/CD discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: Dockerfile best practices (multi-stage builds, layer caching), GitHub Actions patterns, Terraform conventions, deployment safety (canary, blue-green awareness), secrets management, monitoring and alerting, infrastructure as code principles. Research current DevOps tooling and practices.

3. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/data.md — Data engineering and analysis discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: schema design principles, pipeline idempotency, data validation at boundaries, visualization clarity, SQL style, ETL vs ELT awareness, data quality checks, handling missing/null data. Research current data engineering practices.

4. internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/security.md — Security review and hardening discipline. 30-60 lines. Covers: OWASP Top 10 awareness, threat modeling approach, dependency auditing, secrets detection, input validation patterns, authentication/authorization review, security header configuration, vulnerability disclosure. Research current OWASP guidelines and security best practices.

ZERO wolfcastle-specific content in any file.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Non-Language Prompts is complete and correct
